data.description: Shared resolver boundary for both MCP and HTTP rule context flows. `loadGetRulesContext()` caches meta+L0+human-lock data, while `resolveRulesForPath()` builds the canonical rules payload including optional `description_stubs`; `getRules()` adds revision/stale wrapper and audit logging.

Planning batch boundary wraps the same resolver/context as single-path get_rules. It normalizes and deduplicates input paths, then returns per-path entries with deduped rule files, preserving the same revision/stale envelope.

data.naming: Backward compatibility is schema-driven rather than branch-heavy runtime shims: `agentsMetaNodeSchema` derives missing `layer`/`topology_type`, and nodes without `activation` default to path-matching semantics.
data.imports: Server tool wrappers stay thin and typed via `server.registerTool()` + zod `outputSchema`; HTTP adapters should mirror service payloads instead of re-shaping them.
data.formatting: Rules payload uses snake_case transport keys (`revision_hash`, `client_hash`, `human_locked_nearby`, `description_stubs`) consistently across MCP and HTTP.

data.issue: `fab_plan_context` MCP output schema omits `description_stubs`, but the shared resolver now returns them and docs/API clients expect rules-context parity with `fab_get_rules`.
data.impact: Any finalized shared-bundle or payload-unification work must reconcile this schema drift first; otherwise batch planning cannot expose the same resolved bundle view as single-path queries and clients may silently lose description-tier guidance.

data.name: ledger path constants are duplicated by layer
data.description: Server shared code defines LEDGER_FILE as root-level .intent-ledger.jsonl, but separate copies still exist in packages/server/src/http.ts, packages/server/src/api/events.ts, and packages/cli/src/commands/ledger-append.ts. Path convergence needs one resolver or shared export plus legacy fallback semantics.

readLedger/appendLedgerEntry are the main persistence seam for ledger migration. They currently read/write only the root ledger path, and are consumed by doctor, rehydrate-state, approve-human-lock, annotate-intent, API /ledger, and audit correlation.

SSE event streaming tails the ledger file directly with byte offsets and watches the ledger path in WATCHED_PATHS. Any migration must update watcher targets, offset reset behavior, and legacy-read strategy or live Dashboard event playback will silently miss appended ledger events.

pre-commit fast-path explicitly treats .intent-ledger.jsonl as a fabric-managed staged file, and ledger-append stages that same path. Migration must update both the skip heuristic and staged-file filtering, otherwise hook behavior diverges from actual ledger location.

data.issue: Target design is already decided (.fabric/.intent-ledger.jsonl with explicit doctor --fix migration), but current doctor implementation is report-only and no migrate/fix path exists yet. Tests, docs, protected tokens, and CLI messages still encode the legacy root path.
data.impact: A partial path rename will break ledger reads, SSE updates, pre-commit behavior, or docs/i18n consistency unless migration, compatibility reads, and fallout updates ship together.

data.name: rule stable-id precompile via sync-meta
data.description: Rule identity now compiles from leading HTML comment `<!-- fab:rule-id ... -->` into agents meta `stable_id` + `identity_source`; revision hashing includes identity metadata so doctor can flag derived-only nodes and direct fixes back to the rule file header.
